description:
Goal: make the shared native driver and Compiler2 JIT/AOT entry points consume the new Compiler2-owned native handoff end-to-end. Signal: Compiler2 JIT/AOT runs execute through the shared native path without `prepare_preplanned_native`, `materialize_program`, `AbiFacts::derive`, `plan_module_with_role`, or `resolve_module_types`.

Change:
- teach the shared native codegen path to read the new Compiler2-native handoff instead of old planner-owned artifacts
- add Compiler2 JIT/AOT entry helpers and contract tests for quicksort, Enum.reduce, and variadic extern
- remove Compiler2-side dependence on old native-preparation helpers

Why:
- once the native codegen seam has been split cleanly, the backend consumer move should become mechanical instead of reopening architecture work

Blocked by:
- `fz-rh2.8.2.3.4` carves out the planner-free native codegen surface and updates the docs/contract so this ticket resumes against the right boundary

Checks:
- Compiler2 JIT/AOT contract tests pass
- telemetry/assertions prove the forbidden old-world helpers were not used
- callable-entry, marshal, return-lane, and effect contracts survive to native execution
title: Compiler2 native backends: move shared codegen and JIT/AOT entry paths onto the new handoff
